sections.title: What is Cloud Computing (Deep Understanding)
points: Cloud computing is the on-demand delivery of IT resources over the internet with pay-as-you-go pricing.
On-demand means you can provision resources instantly without waiting for hardware setup.
Cloud eliminates the need for upfront investment in servers and data centers.
You can scale resources up or down depending on demand at any moment.
Resources include compute (EC2), storage (S3), databases, networking, AI/ML, and more.
Before AWS, companies had to build and maintain their own data centers.
AWS allows companies to run applications in data centers they do not own.
sections.exam_tip: Keywords: on-demand, scalable, no upfront cost, pay-as-you-go → ALWAYS cloud computing.

sections.title: Cloud Deployment Models (Exam Logic)
points: Cloud deployment: Everything runs in AWS infrastructure.
On-premises: Everything runs locally in your own data center.
Hybrid: Combination of on-premises + cloud.
Hybrid is used for compliance, legacy systems, or regulatory requirements.
Multi-cloud = using multiple cloud providers (less tested but possible).
Hybrid is VERY common in exam questions involving sensitive data.
sections.exam_tip: If question mentions compliance + scaling → answer = Hybrid.

sections.title: 6 Core Benefits of AWS Cloud (Expanded)
points: Trade fixed expense for variable expense → no upfront cost, pay only what you use.
Massive economies of scale → AWS buys hardware in bulk → cheaper for you.
Stop guessing capacity → scale automatically instead of predicting demand.
Increase speed and agility → deploy resources in minutes instead of months.
Stop running data centers → AWS handles maintenance, power, cooling.
Go global in minutes → deploy apps worldwide instantly using Regions.
sections.exam_tip: Capacity problem → Stop guessing capacity. Expansion → Go global in minutes.

sections.title: AWS Global Infrastructure (Important)
points: Region = physical geographic location (e.g., us-east-1, eu-west-1).
Each Region contains multiple Availability Zones (AZs).
Availability Zone = one or more data centers with independent power and networking.
AZs are isolated from each other to prevent failure spread.
Using multiple AZs increases high availability.
Using multiple Regions increases disaster recovery and global performance.
sections.exam_tip: High availability = Multi-AZ. Disaster recovery/global = Multi-Region.

sections.title: High Availability vs Fault Tolerance
points: High Availability = system stays running with minimal downtime.
Fault Tolerance = system continues working even if components fail.
High availability is achieved using multiple AZs.
Fault tolerance requires redundancy at every layer.
AWS infrastructure is designed for both HA and fault tolerance.
sections.exam_tip: If system MUST stay running no matter what → Fault tolerance.

sections.title: AWS Shared Responsibility Model (CRITICAL)
points: AWS = security OF the cloud → hardware, data centers, networking, hypervisor.
Customer = security IN the cloud → OS, applications, data, access control.
Customer must patch operating systems and secure applications.
AWS does NOT access your OS or data.
You control who can access your data.
You are responsible for encryption and permissions.
sections.exam_tip: OS patching, user access, app security → ALWAYS customer.

sections.title: Data Centers (Reality Check)
points: Data centers are physical buildings with servers.
Include power, cooling, networking, and security systems.
AWS manages all physical infrastructure.
Customers never interact with physical servers.
AWS removes the burden of maintaining hardware.
sections.exam_tip: Anything physical (servers, racks, power) → AWS responsibility.

sections.title: Real-World Exam Patterns
points: Global company → use multiple Regions.
High availability → use multiple AZs.
Sensitive data → use Hybrid model.
Scaling problem → use AWS (elastic scaling).
Slow deployment → cloud improves agility.
High cost infrastructure → cloud reduces upfront cost.
sections.exam_tip: Always match scenario → benefit (this is how AWS tests you).

sections.title: Memory Anchors (IMPORTANT)
points: Cloud = On-demand + Internet + Pay-as-you-go.
AWS = Global + Scalable + No infrastructure management.
Regions = global locations.
AZs = redundancy inside region.
Shared Responsibility = AWS (OF) + You (IN).
sections.exam_tip: If stuck → eliminate answers that break these rules.
